programme.title: Gascoigne
programme.desc: Documentary about Paul Gascoigne, which charts his rise to fame from his humble roots in the North East to the world stage and his subsequent personal problems. The film features an extensive interview with the troubled former footballing superstar, as well as comment from those who admired his skills, including Wayne Rooney, Jose Mourinho and Gazza's ex-England and Tottenham Hotspur team-mate Gar

programme.title: My Icon: Chris Hughton
programme.desc: The football manager speaks about the people that inspired his career.

programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story.

programme.title: The Wimbledon Kidnapping
programme.desc: A look back at the mysterious disappearance of Muriel McKay in Wimbledon in 1969, as well as the trial of the first kidnap and ransom case in Britain.

programme.title: The Yogurt Shop Murders: The End of Wondering
programme.desc: A look at the conclusion to the case of the killing of four teenage girls in 1991, when Austin police convened a press conference to announce a break in the investigation just three weeks after the documentary concluded.

programme.title: David Fuller: Monster in the Morgue
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary about the case of David Fuller, a murderer who also sexually abused the bodies of more than 100 women and girls in a hospital mortuary.

programme.title: David Fuller: Monster in the Morgue
programme.desc: The concluding part of the documentary reveals how police made a breakthrough that led them to the man responsible for the murders of two women 33 years earlier.

programme.title: The Armstrong Lie
programme.desc: Documentary following cycling champion Lance Armstrong's fall from grace. Film-maker Alex Gibney was initially commissioned to follow the cyclist as he prepared to make a professional comeback by competing in the Tour de France, only to find himself documenting an unprecedented scandal as Armstrong was exposed as having used performance-enhancing drugs.

programme.title: Rooted
programme.desc: The incredible story of an 800-year-old baobab tree rooted on the savannah of Malilangwe Game Reserve in southern Zimbabwe.

programme.title: Great Parks of Africa
programme.desc: An exploration of the continent's wildlife havens, beginning with a look at Addo Elephant National Park in South Africa.

programme.desc: The team prepares to release two servals, but things go awry when one vanishes into Malawi's dangerous capital. Plus, a monkey arrives in a critical condition.

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Rescue officers Jo and Chantelle free a tangled-up ibis, and vet Mel cares for a baby koala that took a swan dive.

programme.title: America's Wild Seasons
programme.desc: As plunging temperatures bring nature into winter, some animals flee south to find milder climes. But others are determined to tough out the brutal cold.

programme.title: Kalahari: Land of Secret Alliances
programme.desc: A group of scientists test a hypothesis that animal collaboration is the key to the dizzying diversity of the Kalahari's species.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Everyone helps with the upbringing of the little lion cubs in Kenya. Plus, a group of bees start a dance party.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: A fish cleans a shark's pearly white teeth, two giraffes have a cuddle and a monkey shows off his toolbox.

programme.title: Wild Tales from the Farm
programme.desc: As the leaves change, wild urges grip the animals. Rams Roger and Hamish battle for dominance, while Sally the hen flies the coop for a tour of the farm.

programme.title: Wild Tales from the Farm

programme.title: Planet Weird
programme.desc: A look at the great white shark's electrical sensors, the razor-sharp limbs of the dead leaf mantis and the zombie-creating powers of cordyceps fungi.

programme.title: Planet Weird
programme.desc: The partnership between clownfish and anemones, as well as the quid-pro-poo agreements of dung beetles and pitcher plants.

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: The deadly world of snake catching Down Under. Breeding season bites as Mick and Adele rescue a Jack Russel from a python. Mick also corners a red-bellied black snake.

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: Dan and Jess tackle mating eastern browns in a backyard. Mick also finds a venomous snake in couch cushions.

programme.title: Qatar: Pearls in the Sand
programme.desc: The desert peninsula of Qatar, exploring its landscape and inhabitants, as well as its transformation from being a centre for pearls, to being known for oil and gas production.

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Cameras follow four different species of baboons who have conquered African deserts, coasts, savannah and mountains, adapting to the challenges of their environment along the way.

programme.title: Secret Life of the Wombat
programme.desc: A profile of the animal that shares a common ancestor with the koala, but is less well known than its fellow Australian.

programme.title: Secret Life of the Wombat
programme.desc: The wombats' mating season, a break from their solitary lives. Despite their timidness, their behaviour here can be surprisingly energetic.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the Galapagos, a collection of volcanic islands in the Pacific known for its extraordinary wildlife. programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his exploration of the archipelago with a look at how, despite their proximity, no two of the 19 islands are the same. He also uncovers evidence that evolution on Galapagos is occurring faster than Charles Darwin could ever have imagined, and encounters Lonesome George, the last remaining giant Pinta Island tortoise, who died just a few weeks after filming in June 2012.
